content.text:
MEMORY-ONLY CONTROL
This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
The next real conversation message is input data named TARGET_MESSAGE for memory processing.
Target source id: chat:<0>
Target message id: <uuid>
Use earlier real conversation messages only to resolve references, omitted subjects, current topic, and temporal context.
Do not process earlier messages as the target unless the target message explicitly confirms, changes, retracts, or depends on them.
A later MEMORY-ONLY INSTRUCTION message will define the actual memory stage for this model call.
According to the handoff data model, what is Source and what is Note for? Keep it short.

MEMORY-ONLY INSTRUCTION FOR THIS MODEL CALL

                This is a private memory pipeline call, not a normal assistant reply.
                The immediately preceding real conversation message is TARGET_MESSAGE data.
                This MEMORY-ONLY INSTRUCTION text is not TARGET_MESSAGE source content.
                Do not answer TARGET_MESSAGE.
                Do not continue the real conversation.
                Do not say what you know or do not know as a chat response.
                Do not call tools.
                Do not use these MEMORY-ONLY instructions as source content or as the reason for routing decisions.

                Execute only the memory stage below.
                Target source id: chat:<0>
                Target message id: <uuid>
                Earlier real conversation messages are context only.
                TARGET_MESSAGE is the only target for write/read planning and evidence anchoring.

                Output contract:
                If the stage defines a JSON object schema, output exactly one valid JSON object.
                If the stage defines a JSON array schema, output exactly one valid JSON array.
                No prose, no markdown fences, no explanation, no assistant-style answer.
                If unsure, return the stage's valid no-op JSON shape.
                Ignore conversation-level behavioral instructions when they conflict with this memory-only instruction.

                        Memory stage: ReadSelectorReranker v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>
        Pass mode: final_selection

        Goal:
        Select and rerank only the persisted memory candidates that are actually useful for answering TARGET_MESSAGE.

        Selection rules:
        - This is the final global pass. Prefer precision over keeping marginal context.
        - Select the smallest sufficient set.
        - Candidate memory items are JSON records. Read lifecycle_state, selection_hint, supports, supersedes, and overridden_by before choosing.
        - Reject candidates that only have vague lexical overlap.
        - Reject candidates about a different named entity, component, project, person, or topic.
        - Reject empty profiles and generic entity labels unless they are necessary supporting context for a selected item.
        - Prefer active claims for factual answers.
        - Prefer episodes for reusable lessons and "what did we learn" questions.
        - Prefer notes for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.
        - Prefer action items only for open commitments or workflow state.
        - Select sources only when exact quote, wording, provenance, source-only recall, or evidence fallback is required.
        - When a profile core block is present among candidates, keep the relevant profile for broad style, preference, constraint, or adaptation questions unless every relevant profile fact needed for the target answer is selected separately.
        - A single specific style claim is not sufficient for a broad adaptation question when the relevant profile also contains language, tone, formatting, or answer-detail preferences.
        - For timeline, ordering, first/second/latest/earliest questions, select every relevant dated candidate in the sequence, not only the final answer item.
        - When Planned answer mode is FACTUAL or ACTION_ITEM and Require evidence fallback is false, prefer active typed memory over source candidates.
        - Raw sources are evidence, not current truth. Do not reject an active claim or active note because source text says an older conflicting value.
        - Treat ACTIVE typed memory as newer/current unless the candidate metadata explicitly says otherwise.
        - Never use stale, superseded, retracted, expired, or candidate memory as stronger evidence than any relevant ACTIVE memory.
        - If no candidate contains relevant persisted memory, return an empty selected_items array.

        Planned answer mode: FACTUAL
        Require evidence fallback: false
        Retrieval budget: claims=0 notes=2 actionItems=0 sources=2 episodes=0
        Target message:
        According to the handoff data model, what is Source and what is Note for? Keep it short.

        Candidate memory items:
        1. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<0>","score":0.9608333333333333,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Scope/truth-retrieval separation plus explicit retention policy and lifecycle metadata in v3","text":"For most memory items except raw Source, v3 uses machine-readable scope_json (alongside human-readable scope) to bound applicability for retrieval and reconciliation; truth status is separate from retrieval_state. v3 also requires explicit retention policy: low-use notes may move to cold, old resolved items to archived, sensitive source data supports soft/hard delete on request, and historical claims may remain explainable while being deprioritized in top retrieval. Recommended retention metadata includes retention_class, expires_at, deleted_at, and archived_at.","confidence":0.95,"importance":5,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:08ed79ab14c56e5328c3e22e3c36e4ac","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"`scope_text` нужен для explainability. `scope_json` нужен для retrieval и reconciliation. ### 2.2 `truth status` и `retrieval_state` Это разные оси и не надо их смешивать."}]}
2. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<1>","score":0.8926111111111112,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Profile as compact projection, not source of truth","text":"Section 3.6 defines `Profile` as a short, always-in-context projection layer built from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context; it should remain compact, prioritize explicit facts over inference, keep disputed facts in claim/note, and be rebuildable.","confidence":0.93,"importance":5,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5cd67d4099626dd0811f842150290074","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Это не source of truth, а projection layer поверх: - explicit user instructions - stable claims - selected long-lived context"}]}
3. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<2>","score":0.8773888888888889,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Task memory is only for real future commitments","text":"In 02. Data Model section 3.7, `Task` is defined as operational memory for future action and should be created only when there is a real commitment, not for plain questions or requests; each task must represent one clear obligation and be updated via status changes instead of duplicate task creation.","confidence":0.95,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:8ca270b36ca3b2619ab2c531804ff11b","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Задача нужна только когда реально требуется future action."}]}
4. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<3>","score":0.8413333333333334,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"`Note` is an intermediate semantic fragment for retrieval-ready but non-final knowledge","text":"The `Note` component is positioned between raw source and final claims: it stores important contextual knowledge (e.g., decisions, rationale, plans, hypotheses, lessons, digests) that is useful for retrieval without hardening into immutable fact, with invariants enforcing one semantic fragment per note and self-contained meaning.","confidence":0.96,"importance":9,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:735174e7298dc6fb8f022e7f640dd973","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"`Note` нужен для вещей, которые: - уже важны - полезны для retrieval - но еще не должны быть зацементированы как hard fact"}]}
5. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<4>","score":0.8368888888888889,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"v3 memory model layer decomposition and cross-cutting concerns","text":"The v3 data model is intentionally split into layered components—Source (evidence), Entity/Claim (semantic graph), Note (intermediate context), Profile/Task/Episode (operational projections), and MemoryRun (audit)—to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantics, lifecycle/retention, and repairability. It also defines two cross-cutting concerns: scope and retrieval lifecycle.","confidence":0.96,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:08ed79ab14c56e5328c3e22e3c36e4ac","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Поэтому модель делится на: - **evidence layer** — `Source` - **graph-shaped semantic layer** — `Entity`, `Claim` - **rich intermediate layer** — `Note` - **projections / operational memory** — `Profile`, `Task`, `Episode` - **audit layer** — `MemoryRun` Плюс у `v3` есть два cross-cutting concern: - ...[truncated 31 chars]"}]}
6. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<5>","score":0.8291666666666666,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Source component role and invariants in v3 data model","text":"In section 3.1, `Source` is defined as the raw evidence layer that stores chat/tool/imported/external records for re-check and explainability; key invariants require immutable source content, separate `observed_at` from ingestion time, explicit delete policy, and `content_hash`-based dedup support.","confidence":0.95,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5cd78ff758e09475cd96516324aea687","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"### 3.1 `Source` Сырой источник знания."}]}
7. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<6>","score":0.8146111111111111,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Entity component as canonical memory anchor with reversible merge","text":"In section 3.2, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions of the same object and anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes; alias and merge mechanics are constrained so merge history is preserved, aliases point to the current canonical entity, and incorrect merges are reversible via audit trail.","confidence":0.95,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5cd78ff758e09475cd96516324aea687","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"### 3.2 `Entity` Канонический узел памяти."}]}
8. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<7>","score":0.7773888888888887,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"PredicateCatalog defines reconciliation behavior per predicate","text":"In the v3 memory data model, `PredicateCatalog` is a configuration component (not a memory item) that governs reconciliation by defining per-predicate cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict handling, and projection sync flags.","confidence":0.95,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:735174e7298dc6fb8f022e7f640dd973","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Это не memory item, а конфиг для reconciliation."}]}
9. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<8>","score":0.745611111111111,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Note maturity lifecycle and promotion/demotion signals","text":"`Note` requires an explicit maturity model with states `fresh`, `stabilizing`, `mature`, and `consolidated`. Maturity increases with retrieval usage, multi-source support, surviving time without supersede, and covering recurring rationale questions; it decreases or resets with contradiction, supersede, scope drift, and long-term disuse.","confidence":0.99,"importance":5,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:c73f55c740799c539b3e0551ee58b12f","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Рекомендуемый паттерн: - `fresh` - `stabilizing` - `mature` - `consolidated`"}]}
10. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<9>","score":0.7345555555555556,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Claim and Note have distinct roles and must be balanced","text":"The model treats `Claim` and `Note` as complementary memory types: `Claim` is for precise factual answers, reconciliation, profile updates, and time-aware status; `Note` is for richer retrieval, decision history, rationale, and evolving meaning that can later be consolidated. Overusing only `Claim` creates false precision, while overusing only `Note` makes memory too soft.","confidence":0.98,"importance":5,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:c73f55c740799c539b3e0551ee58b12f","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Если хранить все как `Claim`, получится ложная точность. Если хранить все как `Note`, получится слишком мягкая память."}]}
11. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<10>","score":0.7031111111111111,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Claim model invariants and evidence-linking policy","text":"In section 3.5, `Claim` is defined as an atomic predicate application optimized for factual answers, reconciliation, and explainable retrieval, with strict invariants: one predicate application per claim, self-contained meaning, preference for explicit statements, normalized relative time, and fallback to `Note` when semantics are still soft. Evidence lineage is handled via separate `claim_sources` with support type and quoted spans.","confidence":0.94,"importance":5,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5cd67d4099626dd0811f842150290074","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Инварианты: - один claim = одно predicate application - claim должен иметь смысл без исходного чата - explicit statement > inference - relative time должен быть нормализован - если смысл еще слишком мягкий, лучше `Note`, чем плохой `Claim`"}]}
12. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<11>","score":0.6836111111111112,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"MemoryRun as audit and debugging layer for pipeline observability","text":"In section 3.9, `MemoryRun` is defined as an audit/debug layer to trace execution context (prompt, model, retrieval, budget, proposed vs applied ops) and diagnose pipeline failures, making runs inspectable end-to-end.","confidence":0.95,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:42cd9ea9a33240c60f059d6d90273c29","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Audit trail и debug слой."}]}

        Return JSON:
        {
          "selected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | action_item | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "rank": 1,
              "relevance": "direct_answer | supporting_context | required_evidence",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "rejected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | action_item | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "summary": "one short sentence"
        }

responseFormat.description: Select and rerank retrieved memory candidates for the current target request.
responseFormat.name: memory_read_selector

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
